content:
# Depth-Stratified LUT POC Results (2026-04-01)

## Approach
5 depth zones x 33^3 LUTs built from 3 keyframes. Each pixel corrected by blending between nearest depth-zone LUTs. Combined with per-frame depth-aware ambiance grading.

## Results — Best LUT approach so far

| Metric | Depth-LUT vs Full (avg delta) | Previous multi-key (avg delta) |
|--------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| R/G | -0.019 | -0.002 |
| B/G | **+0.001** | +0.005 |
| Saturation | **-0.006** | -0.002 |
| Hue | **+3.4°** | +3.7° |
| Noise | **-14.3pp** (much less noise) | ~-9pp |

## Key improvements
- B/G nearly perfect (0.001 avg delta)
- Noise massively reduced: +31% vs +43-49% full pipeline
- Near-zero white clipping (0.09-0.18%)
- Hue gap narrowed slightly (3.4° vs 3.7°)

## Remaining gap
- R/G still 0.019 short (0.87 vs 0.88 avg) — slightly less warm
- Hue shift still ~3-8° on individual frames
- Speed: 1.2s per frame (5 LUT lookups + depth grading) vs 0.4s for global LUT

## Why depth-stratified helps
Zone-specific LUTs capture that RAUNE-Net corrects far water pixels (blue, low depth) differently from near subject pixels (warmer, high depth). The far-zone LUT preserves blue water, the near-zone LUT warms subjects — matching RAUNE-Net's spatial behavior.
